content.problemStatement:
Sereja and ArraysSereja have an array that consist of n integers a1, a2, ..., an (0 ≤ ai ≤ 1). Sereja can make next operation: 

 fix some integer i (1 ≤ i ≤ n - k + 1)  
subtract 1 from values: ai, ai + 1, ..., ai + k - 1

Sereja call array a good if it is possible to make some operations, that he can and get array that contain only zeros. Now Sereja interested in next question: how many good arrays a with length n exist?






InputFirst line contain integer T — number of testcases. T tests follow. Each testcase is given by two integers n and k.




Constraints  1 ≤ T ≤ 10   1 ≤ k ≤ n ≤ 105  


OutputFor each testcase output answer modulo 109 + 7.




Note
**Test #0-1(25 points) n  ≤ **15


**Test #2(25 points) n  ≤ **100


**Test #3(50 points) n  ≤ **100000
